title: Littlest Helper Tower
summary: A kitchen helper tower with a ladder-rung climb and a raised platform, sized so a toddler can safely stand at counter height — with tip-resistant feet built in.
description:
Two identical ladder-rung ends and a recessed platform are the whole design, but the tip-resistant feet are the part not to skip: a small child standing on a raised platform can put enough leverage on a narrow-footprint tower to tip it backward, and the wider footer at the base is what stops that. The source is right to flag it as a judgment call on the side-to-side resistors — backward tipping is the real risk, so if you're simplifying, that's the corner to cut, not this one.

The platform sits recessed 1" from the outside faces of the tower rather than flush, which does double duty: it hides the platform-support cleats and it keeps the platform physically captured inside the tower instead of resting loose on top of it.

tools.note: Cuts the optional decorative arches; a straight board works without it.

cutList.note: Two arches cut from one board; substitute two 1x3 @ 15" straight boards for a simpler build.
Optional side-to-side resistors — backward tipping is the greater risk.

steps.title: Cut the arches
steps.body: From the 15"-long 1x8, cut two arches with a jigsaw, adjusting the layout so both fit on the one board. A straight 1x3 board across works as a simpler substitute if you'd rather skip this step.

steps.title: Build the ladder rungs
steps.body: Measure and mark the legs for the ladder rung positions. Drill 3/4" pocket holes and glue the ends of the ladder rungs, then attach with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Build two identical leg-and-rung assemblies.

steps.title: Assemble the tower
steps.body: Mark the location of the front and back side pieces and predrill. Glue and screw in place with pocket hole screws, checking for square. Keep the sides open so a child can crawl in and out.

steps.title: Build and install the platform
steps.body: Mark the platform boards 1" in from the outside edges. Attach the platform supports with glue and 1-1/4" screws — these supports both join the platform boards and hold the platform captured inside the tower.

steps.title: Build the tip-resistant feet
steps.body: Round the corners of the tip resistors and screw together. The shorter side-to-side resistors are optional — backward tipping is the greater risk, so build to suit your child's needs.

steps.title: Attach the tip-resistant feet
steps.body:
Screw the tip-resistant footer to the legs.

Sand every surface and finish.

images.alt: Kids kitchen helper tower with ladder rungs and platform
